name: Shrimp Seville
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add onion and garlic; sauté for 3 minutes or until the onion softens.
3. Increase heat to high and add thyme, chili flakes, and shrimp. Cook the shrimp, stirring rapidly, for 1 minute or until they are slightly pink. Remove and set aside.
4. Reduce the heat to medium-low and deglaze the pan with 1 tablespoon of brandy. Allow it to reduce by half.
5. Add white wine and return the sauce to a boil.
6. Chop tomatoes and add them to the skillet, along with saffron if using. Reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for about 15 minutes or until the sauce thickens slightly. The sauce should have the consistency of salsa.
7. Cool the sauce and stir in the reserved shrimp. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Transfer the mixture to a large buttered gratin dish. Sprinkle with Parmesan and dot with butter.
9. Refrigerate for one hour before baking.
10. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes or until the sauce bubbles.
11. Sprinkle with parsley just before serving.
12. Serve directly from the baking dish. Optionally, substitute goat cheese for the Parmesan to add a subtle, interesting flavor.
